political.s: Via parent company Campbell Soup: FEC: $18K PAC donations; 57% to Republican, 43% to Democratic committees.

charity.s: Via parent company Campbell Soup: $8M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.08% of revenue) via Campbell Soup Foundation. Operates the Campbell Soup Foundation: $2.7M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2024, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.focus: Healthier eating, nutrition access, food security, community support

labor.s: Via parent company Campbell Soup: 65 federal penalties totaling $20.1M since 2000 (Violation Tracker).

execPay.s: Via parent company Campbell Soup: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 128:1 (median employee pay $61,506) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2025).

wiki.parent: Madison Dearborn Partners (2005–2012) Campbell Soup Company (2012–2019) Butterfly Equity (2019–present)
wiki.extract: Bolthouse Farms, founded 1915 in Grant, Michigan, is a vertically integrated farm company specializing in refrigerated beverages, salad dressings, and baby carrots. It is located in the San Joaquin Valley of California and is headquartered in Bakersfield, California in Kern County. The company operates facilities in Prosser, Washington. Private equity firm Madison Dearborn Partners owned Bolthouse from 2005 to 2012, when it was bought by the Campbell Soup Company for US$1.55 billion. The company changed hands once again in June 2019, when Campbell sold it to Butterfly Equity.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
